name: TScan Therapeutics, Inc.
business: TScan Therapeutics is a clinical-stage biotechnology company developing T cell receptor (TCR)-engineered T cell therapies for cancer. Its lead candidate TSC-101 targets heme malignancies in patients undergoing hematopoietic cell transplantation. The company also has programs in solid tumors and autoimmune disorders.

exposures.sentence: We are a fully integrated clinical-stage biotechnology company focused on developing a robust pipeline of T cell receptor (TCR)-engineered T cell, or TCR-T, therapies for the treatment of patients with cancer.
While primarily focused on oncology, we believe our target discovery platform is well suited to identify targets that cause T cell-driven autoimmune disorders.

catalysts.sentence: We intend to share early data on these patients and subsequently initiate a registrational trial for TSC-101 using the commercial-ready manufacturing process, pending further feedback from regulatory authorities, in the second quarter of 2026.

catalysts.event: Initiate Phase 1 study for TSC-102 candidates
catalysts.sentence: We plan to initiate a Phase 1 study for both TSC-102 candidates in the second half of 2026.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 5,600 allogeneic HCT procedures are performed yearly in the U.S. in patients with AML and MDS.
tamClaims.sentence: Approximately 5,600 allogeneic HCT procedures are performed yearly in the U.S. in patients with AML and MDS.

tamClaims.claim: Currently, only about 5,600 patients with AML and MDS undergo allogeneic HCT per year in the U.S. out of approximately 35,000 patients diagnosed each year.
tamClaims.sentence: Currently, only about 5,600 patients with AML and MDS undergo allogeneic HCT per year in the U.S. out of approximately 35,000 patients diagnosed each year.

tamClaims.claim: Checkpoint inhibitors have annual sales of about $34 billion in the U.S.
tamClaims.sentence: Despite their efficacy in only a subset of patients, checkpoint inhibitors have annual sales of about $34 billion in the U.S.
